title: Cyclosorus friesii
intro: Cyclosorus friesii, also known as the Fries' leather fern, is a species of fern native to tropical Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce pain, swelling, and oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus friesii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cyclosorus friesii is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus friesii is classified under the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns. This species belongs to the genus Cyclosorus, a genus of ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification is further refined with Cyclosorus friesii being a specific species within this genus. It is also known to be part of the subclass Polypodiidae, which is a subgroup of the class Polypodiopsida. Further classification places it in the division Pteridophyta, which encompasses all ferns.
